title: Solve triangles: angle bisector theorem
description: Use the angle bisector theorem to find missing side lengths in triangles.

assessment_items.question_content:
$\angle DAC=\angle BAD$.

**What is the length of $\overline{AC}$?**  
*Round to one decimal place.*

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]



[[☃ image 1]]
hints: Therefore $AD$ is the bisector of $\angle CAB$

options.alt: Triangle A B C, but angle A is bisected by line segment A D, creating two new triangles, triangle A C D and triangle A B D. Point D is on Side B C. Side A B is five point five units. Side D B is two point eight units. Side C D is three units.

assessment_items.question_content:
